title: Description evidence
principles: Official description text may confirm a named mechanic, activation condition, target behavior, or handling trait that is absent from normalized statistics.
Description evidence must remain qualitative unless the description supplies an explicit value.
Structured fields take precedence when they directly contradict broad promotional wording.
A description may identify mechanics such as chaining, ricochet, homing, lock-on, delayed detonation, damage ramp, conditional reload, or conditional buffs.
Flavor text and comparative claims must not be converted into quantitative rankings.
Character-specific bonuses, external synergies, or conditional transformations should be excluded unless the analysis scope explicitly permits them.
Description-derived mechanics should be marked as descriptive evidence so they are not mistaken for normalized statistics.

structured_confirmation: The description confirms behavior already represented in structured data.
qualitative_extension: The description supplies a relevant qualitative mechanic not represented by numeric fields.
The conclusion must preserve its stated conditions.
promotional_only: The description contains flavor or comparative language without reliable mechanical evidence.
conflicting: Description text and structured data appear inconsistent.
Prefer structured evidence and report uncertainty.
absent: No relevant description evidence is available.

exceptions: Do not infer hidden values from adjectives such as powerful, rapid, accurate, or devastating.
Do not infer a mechanic from the weapon name.
Do not include Warframe-specific bonuses when external synergies are outside scope.
Do not let description text overwrite explicit normalized component data.
